units.content: Text describes a concept distance computation mechanism with process steps
Concept distance decomposes into dimensions showing WHERE texts diverge
Claim dataclass has fields: text, dimension, value, confidence, source_text_index
Common claim dimensions include: mechanism, efficacy, safety, dosage, structure, function, interaction, population, measurement, prediction, definition, classification, comparison
Claims are extracted using LLM with system prompt directing precision and no inference beyond stated text
_extract_claims function parses JSON response from LLM client with error handling for markdown wrapping
_match_claims function uses similarity threshold (0.7) and dimension matching to partition claims
Two claims are shared if they have same dimension and text similarity >= 0.7, or matching numeric values within 5%
Dimensional distance uses Jaccard-like formula: 1.0 - (2 * shared / total)
Quantitative claims with value divergence get blended distance (50% claim-matching, 50% value-divergence)
Scalar distance is computed as RMS of dimensional distances weighted by claim count
Evidence collection prioritizes divergent claims by confidence score, limited to 5 per side
ConceptComparison result includes robustness_score and extractor_agreement only when robustness_mode=True
Robustness mode involves re-running with second extractor and computing cross-extractor agreement on dimensions
